origin: User directive (2026-06-04): the LINKS_TO edge set is dominated by site chrome because harness/indexing/links.py parses <a href> over the WHOLE page and classifies only by URL shape. A proven, DOM-scoped, component-aware extractor exists in ../ema_scraper (parsers/ema_parser.py, EmaPageParser). Port main-content scoping + component-aware extraction into links.py, add DOM context to the edge model, and rebuild ONLY the LINKS_TO edges (no chunk/vector touch). This SUPERSEDES the plain degree-cap hygiene audit and becomes the data-quality prerequisite that Track B's retriever and Track C's IR both depend on.
summary: Replace whole-page URL-shape link extraction with a ported main.main-content-wrapper-scoped, BCL-component-aware extractor; extend ExtractedLink/LINKS_TO with DOM context (link_context, document_type) while preserving kind/anchor + URL normalization; rebuild LINKS_TO edges only and re-measure the degree distribution to verify the ~94% chrome is gone.
supersedes_in_spec: Track B 'is_nav_hub degree-cap blocklist' becomes a secondary safety-net; main-content scoping removes the chrome at the source.
next: /plan or /architect — this explore output is the basis for the planning/architecting step; tasks are outlined in state.json.
